title: geranium reuteri
intro: Geranium reuteri, also known as Mahonia or Oregon grape, is a perennial flowering plant native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The plant contains various medicinal properties, including alkaloids, lignans, and terpenoids, which contribute to its health benefits. The root of the Geranium reuteri plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems, skin conditions, and liver and kidney disorders. Herbal preparations of Geranium reuteri include tinctures, capsules, and teas, and are often used to support immune function and overall well-being.
